title: DeepMind Workers Vote 98% to Unionize - TCR 05/05/26
description:
Google DeepMind workers voted 98% to unionize with the Communication Workers Union and Unite over AI military contracts, Cerebras filed a $26.6 billion IPO with $10 billion in reported order books against $3.5 billion offered, and Greg Brockman testified under oath in Musk v.
Altman that his OpenAI stake is worth $20-30 billion alongside personal investments in Cerebras, CoreWeave, and Helion Energy.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-may-5-2026/

0:00 DeepMind 98% unionization vote
0:43 Open and framing
3:47 Cerebras IPO and the S-1 disclosure
7:10 Onshore wind freeze and AI working group
10:12 Iranian universities and the inverse arc
12:57 Close

This episode covers the DeepMind unionization vote and what "research strikes" mean for frontier AI deployment, the Cerebras S-1 disclosure mapping OpenAI's $1 billion loan and warrant package, the Brockman trial testimony exposing founder cross-ownership, the Trump administration's freeze on 165 onshore wind projects representing 30 GW of capacity, the White House reversal toward federal pre-release review of AI models, Anthropic and OpenAI's separate Wall Street enterprise joint ventures with Blackstone and TPG, and Iranian researchers losing labs and CERN access as bombing damages 30 universities.

The DeepMind vote is the formal endpoint of a tension visible at frontier labs for two years. UK labor law gives the CWU and Unite statutory recognition rights that Google's internal AI principles never had. Eleven days after 600+ Google employees demanded Sundar Pichai reject classified Pentagon contracts - and Google signed the deal anyway - the workers organized into a recognized bargaining unit around exactly that question. The same week, the Cerebras S-1 and Brockman's sworn trial testimony produced a public, document-grade map of frontier AI's internal wiring: warrants, angel positions, debt instruments, and personal stakes that previously lived inside private fundraising decks are now in registration statements and court transcripts. On the federal side, 30 GW of contracted onshore wind sits stalled under a Pentagon review framework that courts already rejected for offshore wind, while a working group for AI pre-release review is being considered six months after the same administration promised the opposite. And in Iran, a philosopher rushed to his Sharif University office after the April 6 strike to find more than 1,000 books reduced to ash - the inverse arc of every other story this week.

- Wired - Greg Brockman Defends $30B OpenAI Stake: 'Blood, Sweat, and Tears': https://www.wired.com/story/greg-brockman-testifies-musk-v-altman-trial/
- Electrek - Trump just blocked 165 US wind projects: https://electrek.co/2026/05/04/trump-just-blocked-165-us-wind-projects-heres-whats-behind-it/
- Nature - 'Heartbreaking': Iranian scientists on losing labs, libraries and liberty: https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-026-01394-y
